# read_vol { #neuroim.read_vol }

```python
read_vol(filename, index=0)
```

Read a 3D neuroimaging volume from file.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name     | Type                                   | Description                                                                    | Default    |
|----------|----------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| filename | [str](`str`) or [Path](`pathlib.Path`) | Path to the neuroimaging file                                                  | _required_ |
| index    | [int](`int`)                           | For 4D files, which 3D volume to extract (0-based) Default is 0 (first volume) | `0`        |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                               | Description          |
|--------|----------------------------------------------------|----------------------|
|        | [DenseNeuroVol](`neuroim.neuro_vol.DenseNeuroVol`) | The loaded 3D volume |

## Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

Unlike R which uses 1-based indexing, Python uses 0-based indexing
for the volume index parameter.

## R Equivalent {.doc-section .doc-section-r-equivalent}

neuroim2::read_vol
